data.title: Apple's  iPhone Stockpile Could Disrupt Supply Chain Amid Looming Chip Tariffs, Warns Top Analyst — Identifies Cupertino's Best Option Beyond White House Talks
data.description: Apple has temporarily avoided steep U.S. tariffs on China-made products, but analyst Ming-Chi Kuo warns the company's preemptive iPhone stockpile and ongoing trade risks could disrupt its supply chain—making India-based production its best long-term safeguard.

data.title: US Stock Futures, Nikkei Climb On Trump's Electronics Tariff Pause: iPhone Parts Makers Surge In Tokyo
data.description: U.S. stock futures are up following President Donald Trump's decision to pause tariffs on consumer electronics products on Sunday, following a whirlwind week for the markets.

data.title: Trump Praises Nayib Bukele For Taking In 'Violent Alien Enemies' Ahead Of Monday Meet Even As US Deports 10 More To El Salvador
data.description: President Donald Trump commended El Salvador’s President Nayib Bukele for accepting custody of dangerous criminals, emphasizing the nations’ joint efforts against terrorism ahead of their meeting at the White House on Monday.

data.title: Michael Saylor Signals Resumption Of Strategy's Bitcoin-Buying Streak After A Week's Break: 'No Tariffs On Orange Dots'
data.description: Michael Saylor, co-founder and executive chairman of Strategy Inc. (NASDAQ:MSTR), hinted at a resumption of its Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) buying streak following a week’s pause.

data.title: US Stocks Likely To Open Higher: Expert Says 'The Selling Pressure Is Starting To Be Exhausted'
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Monday following Friday's advances. Futures of major benchmark indices were higher in premarket.
